title: Check if Bit is Set
description: Tests if bit 4 of R0 is set using TST instruction
code:
; R0 contains input value
 ; Check if bit 4 is set
 TST R0, #0x10 ; Test bit 4 (1<<4)
 BNE BitIsSet ; Branch if Z flag = 0 (bit is set)
 ; Code for bit not set
 B End
BitIsSet:
 ; Code for bit is set
End:
explanation: TST performs bitwise AND (R0 AND 0x10) and sets flags. If bit 4 is set, result is non-zero so Z=0, and BNE (Branch if Not Equal to Zero) is taken.

title: Find Maximum of Two Numbers
description: Uses conditional execution to find maximum without branching
code:
; R0 = first number, R1 = second number
 ; Result will be in R2
 CMP R0, R1 ; Compare R0 and R1, set flags
 MOVGT R2, R0 ; If R0 > R1 (GT), move R0 to R2
 MOVLE R2, R1 ; If R0 <= R1 (LE), move R1 to R2
 ; R2 now contains maximum
explanation: CMP sets flags based on R0-R1. MOVGT executes only when Z=0 and N=V (signed greater). MOVLE executes otherwise. No branch instruction used!

title: Save and Restore CPSR
description: Shows how to save CPSR to memory and restore it
code:
; Save CPSR
 MRS R0, CPSR ; Move CPSR to R0
 STR R0, [SP]! ; Push to stack
 
 ; Change to Supervisor mode (if not already)
 MSR CPSR_c, #0xD3 ; 0xD3 = 10010011 (IRQ disabled, Supervisor mode)
 
 ; ... do supervisor tasks ...
 
 ; Restore CPSR
 LDR R0, [SP], #4 ; Pop from stack
 MSR CPSR_all, R0 ; Restore CPSR from R0
explanation: MRS reads CPSR into general register. MSR with _c suffix writes only control bits. MSR_all writes all 32 bits including flags.

title: Signed Number Classification
description: Classifies a number as positive, negative, or zero
code:
; R0 contains number to classify
 ; Returns: R0 = 0 (zero), 1 (positive), 2 (negative)
 
Classify:
 CMP R0, #0 ; Compare with zero
 BEQ IsZero ; Branch if Z=1 (equal to zero)
 
 ; Not zero - check sign
 BMI IsNegative ; Branch if N=1 (negative)
 
IsPositive:
 MOV R0, #1 ; Return 1 for positive
 B Done
 
IsNegative:
 MOV R0, #2 ; Return 2 for negative
 B Done
 
IsZero:
 MOV R0, #0 ; Return 0 for zero
 
Done:
explanation: CMP sets flags: Z if zero, N if negative (MSB=1), clears both if positive. BMI checks N flag. Uses conditional branches for classification.

title: Range Check using Flags
description: Checks if a value is within a specified range
code:
; R0 = value to check
 ; R1 = lower bound, R2 = upper bound
 ; Returns: R3 = 1 if in range, 0 if out of range
 
 CMP R0, R1 ; Compare with lower bound
 BLT OutOfRange ; If value < lower, out of range
 
 CMP R0, R2 ; Compare with upper bound
 BGT OutOfRange ; If value > upper, out of range
 
 ; Value is in range
 MOV R3, #1
 B Done
 
OutOfRange:
 MOV R3, #0
 
Done:
explanation: First CMP checks lower bound (BLT = signed less than, N!=V). Second CMP checks upper bound (BGT = signed greater than, Z=0 and N=V). Both must pass for valid range.
